text.title: pwa-development Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for pwa-development as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs default to CacheFirst everywhere; that breaks freshness for APIs and HTML.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Review per-route strategy. CacheFirst is only appropriate for long-lived assets like images and fonts.

failure-mode.description: Agents forget the offline fallback registration in the SW install event, leaving navigations dead offline.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always register /offline.html in the SW and use it as the navigateFallback for all navigations.

failure-mode.description: Service-worker scope errors: registering at /app/sw.js but expecting root scope /.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SW scope is limited to the directory of the SW file; /app/sw.js cannot cache / paths. Place SW at root.

failure-mode.description: iOS gotcha: agents test on Chromium and miss Safari quirks (no Background Sync, limited Push, storage caps).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Test explicitly on Safari iOS with DevTools to verify functionality. Do not assume iOS behavior matches Chrome.

failure-mode.description: Push: agents generate VAPID keys but skip the subscription persistence layer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require an explicit DB schema diff for storing push subscriptions. Push endpoints must be persisted and renewed.
